subject: Biology
title: Cancer: Division Gone Wrong
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Cancer: Division Gone Wrong.

hook.text: Cancer starts when a cell ignores the signals that say 'stop dividing.'

buildup.text: Mutations in growth genes break the brakes. The cell multiplies without limit.

discovery.text: A tumor is a mass of rogue cells consuming resources and crowding healthy tissue.

twist.text: One mutation isn't enough. Several must accumulate—often taking years or decades.

climax.text: Chemotherapy targets rapidly dividing cells, exploiting cancer's own reckless growth.

punchline.text: Cancer is a cell that forgot how to stop.

quiz.question: What causes a cell to become cancerous?
